mechanism_narrative: A low level of the federal funds rate indicates an accommodative monetary policy stance, which typically supports equity valuations by lowering discount rates and encouraging economic activity, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in > 55% of periods where FEDFUNDS is below 2.0% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market and broader economic weakness, which typically leads to reduced corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by 0.5 percentage points or more over a 3-month period
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in > 50% of instances where UNRATE rises by 0.5pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Strong and sustained growth in both retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and investment, signaling a healthy economic expansion that drives corporate revenue growth and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.5% AND HOUST 3-month average growth > 5%
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to produce positive 20d returns in more than 50% of triggered periods over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production in an environment of contained inflation suggests healthy supply-side expansion without overheating, leading to improved corporate profitability and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized growth > 3% AND CPIAUCSL 12-month change < 2.5%
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in > 45% of periods where industrial production is strong and inflation is low.

mechanism_narrative: Robust quarterly GDP growth signifies a strong overall economic environment, which typically translates into higher corporate earnings and investor confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative in more than 50% of instances following a quarter with GDPC1 growth above 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent job creation, as indicated by strong payroll employment growth, reflects a healthy labor market and robust consumer spending capacity, which supports corporate revenues and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in > 55% of periods where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150,000 for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high and accelerating consumer price inflation erodes purchasing power, increases input costs for businesses, and often prompts tighter monetary policy, all of which negatively impact corporate profits and S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in > 40% of periods when CPIAUCSL 12-month change exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low unemployment and strong retail sales indicates robust consumer health and spending, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and profits, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE below 4.0% AND RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.3%
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where both unemployment is low and retail sales growth is strong.
